label: c01
approach: Classic tuples: headX/headY + foodX/foodY + tail[] $state, DX/DY dir arrays, board via nested Array.from.

label: c02
approach: Flat single-index cells (i=y*6+x); snake = index array (head first), food = index; board built by per-row glyph concatenation over a single 0..35 loop.

label: c03
approach: Single packed $state object {snake,food,dir,next,playing,high}; snake as flat cell indices (y*6+x); board/score/best/playing via $derived.

label: c04
approach: Single snake[][] (head at [0]) + food[]; single-pass string-first board build; inline array.some collisions; 4 $state.

label: c05
approach: Snake as flat cell-index array (y*6+x), head at index 0; unshift new head, pop unless eating; render snake[0]=player, includes()=tail. State: snake, food, dir, next, playing, high.

label: c06
approach: Plain insertion-ordered Set of "x,y" body keys + manually recomputed $state board (Set non-reactive; oldest segment dropped via Set order).

label: c08
approach: Direction as [dx,dy] vector (cd/nd); single snake tuple array (head at index 0); reversal via component negation vs committed dir.

label: c09
approach: Snake as single index-array (head-first, i=y*6+x); board/score/best/occ all $derived, tiny imperative move().

label: c10
approach: Index-based cells (one int per cell, tail=number[]), setInterval tick, $effect setup; aggressive ternary/operator golf.

label: c11
approach: Single flat-index snake list (head-first); board via nested Array.from/map+join closures.

label: c12
approach: Flat-index snake (head + tail as 0..35 indices); board = 36-slot bg array, paint tail/food/head, slice into 6 rows.
